steps.title: Identify the like terms
steps.content: The terms $4yz^3$ and $3yz^3$ are like terms because they have the exact same variable part, $yz^3$. The term $5yz$ is not like them because it has $z$, not $z^3$.

steps.title: Combine coefficients only
steps.content:
Add the coefficients of the like terms and keep the variable part unchanged:
$$4yz^3+3yz^3=7yz^3$$
So the full expression becomes:
$$4yz^3+5yz+3yz^3=7yz^3+5yz$$

steps.title: Check with a Desmos table
steps.content:
To avoid the common exponent trap, test the original and choice A at two $(y,z)$ values:
$$y_1=[1,2]$$
$$z_1=[2,3]$$
$$o_1=4y_1z_1^3+5y_1z_1+3y_1z_1^3$$
$$a_1=7y_1z_1^3+5y_1z_1$$
The lists match, so choice A is equivalent.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A is $7yz^3+5yz$. Choices B, C, and D incorrectly add exponents or combine unlike terms. Therefore A is correct.
